title: Orchis carpetana
taxonomy: Orchis carpetana is a species of orchid in the genus Orchis. The classification of Orchis carpetana is as follows: it belongs to the family Orchidaceae, subfamily Orchidoideae, tribe Orchideae, subtribe Orchidinae, and it is a member of the genus Orchis. The genus Orchis is further divided into several sections, with O. carpetana being part of the section Orchis. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and molecular phylogenetic studies. The classification of Orchis carpetana is still being refined as new research emerges.
uses: Orchis carpetana, also known as the Red helleborine, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. intro: Orchis carpetana, also known as the green-winged orchid, is a species of terrestrial orchid native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties and has been used to treat a variety of health issues, including inflammation, respiratory problems, and cardiovascular conditions, due to its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The bioactive constituents of Orchis carpetana include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Orchis carpetana are tinctures, infusions, and capsules, often used to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and other stress-related conditions.
